query: How many cruise ships have come to the city?

response.headline: German Cruise Visitors Nearly Quadrupled Since 2002
response.lede: Every summer, ships line up at the harbor, bringing thousands of visitors from across the world. But who actually steps ashore, and how has this changed over time? The numbers reveal that German cruise passengers alone surged from about 13,000 in 2002 to nearly 49,000 in 2019.

story_blocks.body: That’s nearly four times as many as in 2002, showing remarkable growth.

story_blocks.heading: German Arrivals Soared, Hitting a New High in 2019
story_blocks.body: In the chart below, you’ll see a steady rise in visitors from Germany over nearly two decades. The numbers start at 13,000 in 2002 and climb each year, peaking at almost 49,000 in 2019. This jump of about 280% shows how popular the city has become with German cruise guests.

story_blocks.heading: Dutch Visitors Rose, Then Fell Back From Their Peak
story_blocks.body: The next chart shows cruise passenger numbers from the Netherlands. Dutch arrivals grew from just over 500 in 2002 to a high point of 1,400 in 2008. But since that peak, the numbers dipped back to about 1,200 by 2019, showing some ups and downs along the way.

story_blocks.heading: Russian Cruise Traffic Went Down Over Time
story_blocks.body: Now, take a look at the third chart. Unlike other countries, arrivals from Russia started at about 550 in 2002, but dropped by 40% by 2019. After peaking at 660 in 2005, the numbers fell to just 330 by the end of the period. Not every country followed the same pattern.

story_blocks.heading: Visitors Arrived From Across Europe and Beyond
story_blocks.body: Other nationalities, like Swiss and Austrian cruise visitors, also saw big increases over the years. Numbers from Switzerland grew fivefold, and Austrian arrivals topped out in 2012. This tells us that the city’s cruise business grew not just from one country, but from many parts of Europe.

story_blocks.heading: What This Means for Life in the City
story_blocks.body: These changing patterns reflect how our city has become a magnet for cruise travelers. More visitors mean a livelier harbor and busier local businesses during the tourist season. The mix of guests also brings different cultures to our streets, making the city feel even more like an international meeting point.

response.data_note: This data tracks the number of cruise ship passengers passing through the city’s harbors from various countries each year, from 2002 to 2019. It doesn’t count the number of ships themselves, only the passengers, and breaks totals down by nationality. More recent years and detailed passenger information are not included. As the source's licence information is missing, this story is based on data made publicly available by city authorities—interpret it as a broad view of trends, not an official count for every ship.
response.followup_question: How much do tourists spend in the city each year?
